name: L. A. Mission
description_short: LA Mission Federal Credit Union is an NCUA-insured credit union serving Santa Rosa Church members and their families, offering auto loans, personal loans, and share accounts.
description_long:
LA Mission Federal Credit Union (LAMFCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution operating out of San Fernando, California. The credit union maintains NCUA insurance, positioning it as a federally-regulated alternative to traditional banks. Founded to serve the Santa Rosa Church community, LAMFCU has built a focused membership model requiring applicants to be registered parishioners, employees, or students at Santa Rosa Church, or have an immediate family member who is already a member.

LAMFCU offers a range of financial products designed for personal and auto lending. Their primary services include new and used auto loans (starting as low as 6.49% APR), personal signature loans (from 11.00% APR), and share loans backed by savings deposits (3.49% APY as of February 2023). The credit union also provides money order services through MoneyGram at $1.00 per order, along with online banking and bill payment capabilities. Their lending products are marketed as accessible alternatives to traditional banks with competitive rates.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its community-based membership structure and competitive rates on auto financing. The share loan product—allowing borrowers to access funds up to their deposit amounts at low interest rates—represents a unique offering compared to standard personal loan products. LAMFCU operates with limited hours and a single physical location, reflecting its niche market focus rather than attempting broad regional reach.

While LAMFCU offers legitimate credit union services with favorable rates on auto loans and share-backed lending, prospective members should note the restrictive membership eligibility requirements. The organization's limited operating hours (closed Sunday and Monday, operating 10 AM–4:30 PM most weekdays) and single San Fernando location may pose accessibility challenges. Additionally, the website contains minor spelling errors and technical issues, which may indicate resource constraints typical of smaller financial institutions.
pros: Auto loan rates as low as 6.49% APR, competitive with larger financial institutions
Share loans offer 3.49% APY, the lowest-cost borrowing option for members with deposits
NCUA-insured member accounts provide federal deposit protection
Online banking and bill payment capabilities available to members
Membership-based structure aligns institution incentives with member interests rather than shareholders
Personal loans available without collateral requirement
MoneyGram money orders available at low $1.00 fee for bill payment convenience
cons: Restrictive membership eligibility—must be Santa Rosa Church parishioner, employee, student, or have an immediate family member as existing member
Limited operating hours (closed Sunday and Monday; Tuesday–Thursday 10 AM–4:30 PM only; Friday until 5 PM; Saturday 9 AM–3 PM) reduce accessibility
Single physical location in San Fernando, California limits in-person service accessibility for non-local members
Personal loan rates starting at 11.00% APR are substantially higher than auto loan rates, limiting affordability for unsecured borrowing
Website contains spelling errors and broken links (support email shows placeholder domain), suggesting limited technical resources
best_for: Santa Rosa Church members or their immediate families seeking competitive auto financing
Individuals with existing savings deposits who want low-cost borrowing through share-backed loans
San Fernando area residents needing personal or auto loans with online banking convenience
diagnosis: LA Mission FCU is best for Santa Rosa Church community members who meet strict eligibility requirements and seek competitive auto financing or low-cost borrowing against savings deposits. The primary limitation is highly restricted membership access—individuals without church affiliation or existing member family connections cannot join, making this exclusively a community credit union rather than a general-purpose lender.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: L. A. Mission — Credit Union in San Fernando, CA | CreditDoc
meta_description: L. A. Mission — NCUA-insured credit union in San Fernando, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
